title: At-the-bit mud loss treatment
abstract: The invention is a while drilling mud loss treatment method comprises providing a drilling tool main body (1) with a through bore (101) connected to an above arranged wired drill pipe string (0) with a communication line (01) to a topsides monitoring and control system (02), the drilling tool main body (1) connected to a below arranged one or more drill collar sections (2) with a lower of said drill collar sections (2) connected to a drill bit (3), and drilling in a well. The main body (1) is provided with an annular tank (10) with a swellable sealant (5) and the annular tank (10) has a valve (12) to an outlet (120) to the through bore (101). A control system in the main body (1) receives MWD sensor signals from an MWD sensor system (11) and controls the valve (12) having a valve actuator (121). The control system (13) is, during drilling, running a monitoring and control algorithm (131) using the signals as input for detecting an undesired mud loss state during drilling, and, if a mud loss state is detected, to command said valve actuator (121) to open said valve (12) upon detecting an undesired mud loss state, so as for ejecting said swellable sealant (5) to said through bore (101).
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A mud loss treatment drilling tool comprising: - a main body (1) with a through bore (101), and an upper tooljoint (101U) to connect to an above arranged drill pipe string (0) with an associated communication line (01) to and from a topsides monitoring and control system (02), and a lower tooljoint (101L) to connect to a below arranged one or more drill collar sections (2) with a lower of said drill collar sections (2) connected to a drill bit (3) with nozzles, - said main body (1) provided with an annular tank (10) with a swellable sealant (5), - said annular tank (10) having an outlet (120) to said through bore (101), and a valve system (12) having a valve actuator (121, 128) controlled by a control system (13) receiving MWD sensor signals from a MWD sensor system (11) with an algorithm (131) arranged for monitoring , wherein said control system (13) and said MWD sensor system (11) are powered by an energy source (14), wherein said control system (13) and said MWD sensor system (11) are arranged within said main body (1), - said control system (13) having an algorithm (131) arranged for monitoring and processing at least said MWD sensor signals in order to detect an undesired mud loss state, said control system (13) further arranged for commanding said valve actuator (121, 128) to allow said annular tank (10) ejecting said swellable sealant (5) to said through bore (101) upon said algorithm (131) having detected such an undesired mud loss state, - said swellable sealant (5) is arranged for, when ejected from said outlet (120), to contact said drilling mud in said through bore (101), to start reacting to swell during the time it takes said drilling mud with said swellable sealant (5) to pass out through the drilling bit nozzles, and continue to swell and set while said drilling mud being placed in a fracture extending from a borehole being drilled by said drill bit (3). 2. The mud loss treatment tool of claim 1, - said valve system (12) comprising a valve actuator (123) for an inlet valve (124) controlled by said control system (13) for allowing drilling fluid in from the through bore (101) and - said valve actuator (121) for activating an outlet valve (12, 121) to release said swellable sealant (5) to said main bore (101) . 3 . The mud loss treatment tool of claim 1, - said annular tank (10) having 

 - said valve actuator (128) for a diverter valve (127) arranged near an upper end of said annular tank (10) in saidthrough bore (101) to divert drilling mud to an upper end of said annular tank (10) through an inlet (129), preferably with a shear membrane to said annular tank (10), and said outlet (120) arranged near a lower end of said annular tank (10) back to said through bore (101), so as for flushing said swellable sealant (5) into said through bore (101). 4. The mud loss treatment drilling tool of any of the preceding claims 1 to 2, said annular tank (10) provided with a piston (126) arranged for driving out said swellable sealant (5) to said valve system (12). 5. The mud loss treatment drilling tool of claim 4, said annular tank (10) provided with an inlet valve or pump (124) with a mud balancing pressure inlet from said through bore (101) near an upper end of said annular tank (10) and provided with an inlet valve actuator (123), said pump or valve contributing to driving said piston (126). 6. The mud loss treatment drilling tool of any of the preceding claims, wherein - said MWD sensor system (11) is provided with one or more of internal bore sensors (111) and / or annulus sensors (112), such as: - bore mud pressure (lllPb) sensor - bore mud flow velocity (lllVb) sensor (ultrasound Doppler) - annulus mud pressure (112Pa) sensor, mud pressure bore/annulus differential pressure (Pb-Pa) sensor - annulus mud velocity (112Va) sensor (ultrasound Doppler) - bore temperature (111Tb) sensor - annulus temperature (112Ta) sensor, temperature bore/annulus differential temperature (Ta-Tb) sensor - bore mud density (lllRho b) sensor - annulus mud density (112Rho a) sensor. 7. The mud loss treatment drilling tool of any of the preceding claims, wherein - said control system (13) arranged for communicating a signal representing said undesired mud loss state via a downhole modem (15) to a surface modem (415) to said topside monitoring and control system (02), so as for receiving a surface triggering command signal to said valve actuator (121) to open said valve system (12) for ejecting said swellable sealant (5). 8. The mud loss treatment drilling tool of any of the preceding claims, wherein said communication line (01) associated with said drilling pipe string (0) is a wired line of said drilling pipe string (0), said wired line connected between said tool (1) and a topsides monitoring and control system (02). 9. The mud loss treatment drilling tool of any of the preceding claims, wherein said communication line (01) associated with said drilling pipe string (0) is a mud pulse telemetry system in the annulus of said drilling pipe string (0), said mud pulse telemetry system signalling between said tool (1) and a topsides monitoring and control system (02). 10. A while drilling mud loss treatment method comprising: - providing a drilling tool main body (1) with a through bore (101), - to an above arranged drill pipe string (0) with a communication line (01) to a topsides monitoring and control system (02); - said drilling tool main body (1) connected to a below arranged one or more drill collar sections (2) with a lower of said drill collar sections (2) connected to a drill bit (3), and drilling in a well; - said main body (1) provided with an annular tank (10) with a swellable sealant (5), - said annular tank (10) having a valve system (12) to an outlet (120) to said through bore (101), - said main body (1) having a control system (13) receiving MWD sensor signals from an MWD sensor system (11) in said main body (1), both receiving energy from an energy source (14) in said main body (1), said control system (13) controlling said valve system (12) having a valve actuator (121, 128), - the control system (13) is, during drilling, running a monitoring and control algorithm (131) receiving the MWD sensor signals as input for detecting an undesired mud loss state during drilling, and, if a mud loss state is detected, to command said valve actuator (121) to activate said valve system (12) upon detecting an undesired mud loss state, so as for ejecting said swellable sealant (5) to said through bore (101). - said swellable sealant (5) is arranged for, when ejected from said outlet (120) to said drilling mud, to start reacting with said drilling mud to swell during the time it takes said drilling mud with a portion of said swellable sealant (5) to pass through the drilling bit nozzles, and continue to swell and set while said drilling mud being placed in a fracture extending from a borehole being drilled by said drill bit (3). 11. The while drilling mud loss treatment method of any of the preceding claims 10 - 11, wherein 

 - said control system (13) sends a signal (SL) representing said detected undesired mud loss state via a downhole modem (15) to a surface modem (415) to saidtopsides monitoring and control system (02), and awaits receiving a surface triggering confirmation command signal (SC) down to said valve actuator (121) to activate said valve system (12) for allowing the tank (10) to ejecting said swellable sealant (5). 12. The while drilling mud loss treatment method of any of the preceding claims 10 - 11, wherein - after having ejected said swellable sealant (5) and said swellable sealant (5) has halted said undesired mud loss, circulating out remaining swellable sealant (5) not having entered into fractures. 13. The while drilling mud loss treatment method of any of the preceding claims 10 - 12, continuing drilling beyond the well depth in which said undesired mud loss state occurred. 14. The while drilling mud loss treatment method according to any of the preceding claims 10 - 13, while having determined at the surface the presence of an undesired mud loss status, sending said surface triggering confirmation command signal (SC) down to said valve actuator (121), the method comprising making a pre-determined series of drill string rotations, selectable among one or more of the following: - halting rotation and reversing it, - conducting a series of RH and LH rotations - conducting a series of RH rotations interrupted by periods of halt of rotations - increasing WOB and decreasing WOB in a predetermined pattern. - running at a predefined rotation speed RPM for a pre-determined period of time. 15. The while drilling mud loss treatment method according to any of claims 10 - 14, comprising - while having determined at the surface the presence of an undesired mud loss status, also based on measurements conducted on the surface such as downgoing drilling mud volume rate, returning drilling mud volume rate, sending said surface triggering confirmation command signal (SC) down to said valve actuator (121). 16. The while drilling mud loss treatment method according to any of the claims 10 - 15, comprising sending signals such as the signals from the tools to the surface, or signals representing surface- based measurements, or said surface triggering confirmation command signal (SC) on wired pipe string.
